subtopics.label: Great Lakes 100-Mile Border Zone Debate
subtopics.attitude: Skeptical, critical, and frustrated with jurisdictional definitions and accuracy
subtopics.summary: This cluster discusses the application and accuracy of the '100 miles' border zone, particularly how it affects areas around the Great Lakes. Users question how the 100-mile proximity is measured and applied, noting that locations near a lake's coastline might be considered within 100 miles of the border, even if further from the actual border line [3lmg7iz4tpk2g]. There's also debate over whether cities like Chicago fall within this 100-mile US border jurisdiction, despite not being near the Canadian border or an ocean [3lgct6gxtv22n]. Furthermore, there is strong frustration and disagreement regarding the accuracy of maps or measurements depicting 100-mile distances within Michigan and across Lake Michigan [3m2n5rqfhls23].

key_quotes.quote: Chicago is not within 100 mi of Canada or one of the oceans, but it's still within 100 miles of the US border.
key_quotes.note: This quote highlights the specific interpretation of the 100-mile border zone applying to major Great Lakes cities.

key_quotes.quote: the Border is within "100 miles" of the border, which isn't actually what they do
key_quotes.note: This reflects confusion and skepticism about how the 100-mile border rule is practically implemented and measured.

key_quotes.quote: This map is so effing wrong measuring 100 miles that whoever posted it or made it is freaking insane.
key_quotes.note: This phrase captures the strong frustration and disbelief regarding the perceived inaccuracy of geographical representations of distance.

subtopics.label: Lake Michigan's Canadian Border Status
subtopics.summary: This cluster addresses the geographical relationship between Lake Michigan and Canada, specifically debating or clarifying whether they share a border. Some users emphatically state that Lake Michigan does not border Canada [3m2n22wstbs2l], while others present this information in the form of a trivia question [3mfucoprtq224]. There is also a playful, speculative comment about smuggling across Lake Michigan in relation to Canada [3lzqxaq2jlk2k].

key_quotes.quote: LAKE MICHIGAN IS NOT ON A BORDER WITH CANADA!
key_quotes.note: This quote directly asserts the central geographical fact being discussed in the cluster, correcting a common misconception.

key_quotes.quote: What is the only one of the Great Lakes that does not border Canada
key_quotes.note: This quote represents the topic being framed as a trivia question, indicating a common point of discussion or inquiry.

key_quotes.quote: smuggle hooch across Lake Michigan, they can smuggle futons. O Canada!
key_quotes.note: This quote offers a humorous, speculative perspective, hinting at a perceived, albeit indirect, connection or proximity between Lake Michigan and Canada.

subtopics.label: Confusion over Great Lakes' 100-mile Border Zone
subtopics.summary: People are discussing how the U.S. '100-mile Border' concept applies to and encircles the Great Lakes [3mcduiwip2s2t]. There is significant confusion about how cities like Chicago, situated on Lake Michigan (an entirely U.S. lake), are nevertheless considered to be within this 100-mile CBP enforcement zone [3mabapwrf522e]. This confusion extends to the idea that Lake Michigan itself is 'still considered a border' by some, leading to questions about the zone's specific application [3lzwh5ivqks2l].

key_quotes.quote: The 100-mile U.S. "Border" also encircles the Great Lakes.
key_quotes.note: This quote introduces the central topic: the existence and application of the 100-mile border zone around the Great Lakes.

key_quotes.quote: Don’t understand, but it is in CBP 100 mile enforcement zone
key_quotes.note: This phrase captures the prominent sentiment of bewilderment and lack of clarity regarding how the border zone is defined and enforced.

key_quotes.quote: lake Michigan is still considered a border I guess.
key_quotes.note: This quote illustrates the uncertainty and speculative reasoning users employ to explain why areas around Lake Michigan fall within the border zone.

subtopics.label: Lake Michigan's Entire US Location
subtopics.summary: This cluster discusses the unique geographic status of Lake Michigan in relation to national borders. Users are clarifying that Lake Michigan is the only one of the Great Lakes located entirely within the United States [3m2n2nadtbc2x, 3m2n2c7hdvc2x]. Consequently, there is no Canadian border with the United States in Lake Michigan, distinguishing it from the other four Great Lakes [3m2n2nadtbc2x, 3m2n2c7hdvc2x]. The discussion also includes questions about whether Lake Michigan is a US "border" given its interior location [3mdg2d2l4zk2w].

key_quotes.quote: the only one of the Great Lakes located entirely within the United States
key_quotes.note: This quote succinctly states the central fact being discussed regarding Lake Michigan's unique geographic position.

key_quotes.quote: There is no Canadian border with the United States in Lake Michigan
key_quotes.note: This phrase directly addresses the border question that prompts much of the discussion in this cluster.

key_quotes.quote: Isn't it entirely within the United States?
key_quotes.note: This quote represents the questions or clarifications sought by users regarding Lake Michigan's location, indicating a common point of discussion.

subtopics.label: Lake Michigan International Border Confusion
subtopics.summary: Users are discussing how the Great Lakes generally serve as an international border with Canada [3lxzop3fgqs2g]. A specific point of debate and confusion centers on Lake Michigan's status, with some users questioning or struggling to understand how it could be considered an "international border" [3mbzefg36ss2d, 3mdg3y5k74k2h]. This indicates a lack of clarity regarding international boundaries within the Great Lakes system.

key_quotes.quote: I still don’t understand how Lake Michigan is an “international border”
key_quotes.note: This quote directly expresses the central confusion regarding Lake Michigan's status as an international border.

key_quotes.quote: The Great Lakes get you to an international border (Canada)
key_quotes.note: This post provides a general statement that sets the context for the Great Lakes as international borders.

key_quotes.quote: so are the great lakes except michigan.
key_quotes.note: This statement highlights the specific exclusion or distinction of Lake Michigan from the other Great Lakes acting as borders.

subtopics.label: Questioning Border Patrol near Chicago despite distance
subtopics.attitude: Skeptical and questioning of Border Patrol jurisdiction
subtopics.summary: Discussions in this cluster revolve around the significant distance separating Chicago from the Canadian border, with distances cited as "over 400 miles" and "314 miles away" [3m4l4kk5ddc23, 3mdgc5lirzs2w]. Users are questioning the legality and presence of Border Patrol in Chicago, emphasizing that Lake Michigan, which Chicago borders, is "100% American" and far removed from Canada [3m4l4kk5ddc23, 3mdgc5lirzs2w]. The general sentiment is that it's "a haul from Chicago to the Canadian border" [3lzovi7rdrs2h].

key_quotes.quote: Why is Border Patrol even legal in Chicago?
key_quotes.note: This quote directly expresses the core skepticism and questioning of Border Patrol's presence and authority.

key_quotes.quote: Lake Michigan, which Chicago borders, is 100% American.
key_quotes.note: This highlights the perceived lack of a 'border' issue in Chicago itself, reinforcing the questioning of federal patrol.

key_quotes.quote: It is over 400 miles from Chicago.
key_quotes.note: This succinctly captures the central theme of the considerable distance involved, which underpins the discussion.
